info.title: B2W Ops API
info.description:
General Information

The OpsAPI framework is a .NET Core based architecture to support one or more RESTful Web API endpoints to interact with the B2W Operational Suite.

All controllers in the framework conform to the .NET ApiController behavior and support HTTP verb-based endpoints augmented with attribute-based request routing.

Every endpoint is asynchronous in nature and returns an instance of IActionResult. Depending on the intent of an endpoint, it will either return just a status code, or a status code with relevant data.

No endpoint allows exceptions, if any, to “leak” to the .NET CLR. All exceptions which are propagated by the endpoints are centrally caught, reported, and handled. Exception information is logged to a log file, located in the Logs folder within the deployment folder.

The following sections provide useful information regarding configuration, and usage/testing guidelines. Click on the section title to expand/collapse that section. When you hover the mouse over a section, if the cursor changes to a pointer (a hand with a pointing-finger), you may expand/collapse that section.

Configuration

Once installed, OpsAPI may be configured using its configuration file appsettings.json. This is not typically necessary: the installer sets appropriate values. Caution must be exercised in editing the configuration file, as errors may lead to application misbehavior.

Following configuration values are relevant:

  • Settings
    • RequireTLS
      • When set to true: The application will NOT take traffic on HTTP, but only on HTTPS.
      • When set to false: The application will take traffic on HTTP. This is the default.
    • PageSize
      Maximum number of items that will be returned for each invocation of a GET request to retrieve data. The default is 100.
    • Logging
      • IncludeStackTrace
        • When set to true: The system will include the stack trace when logging system exceptions.
      • LogRetentionDays
        • Determines the number of days of log files that the system will retain.
      • LogLevel
        • Default: Set to one of the following values:
          Level Usage
          Verbose Verbose is the noisiest level, rarely (if ever) enabled for a production app.
          Debug Debug is used for internal system events that are not necessarily observable from the outside, but useful when determining how something happened.
          Information Information events describe things happening in the system that correspond to its responsibilities and functions. Generally these are the observable actions the system can perform.
          Warning When service is degraded, endangered, or may be behaving outside of its expected parameters, Warning level events are used.
          Error When functionality is unavailable or expectations broken, an Error event is used.
          Fatal The most critical level, Fatal events demand immediate attention.
    • MaxContentLength
      Maximum size (in bytes) of the body content while uploading attachments. The default is 10 MB.
  • ConnectionString
    • DataContext
      SQL Server database connection string for the B2W Ops database.
Usage Guidelines

All CRUD endpoints are secured using Bearer Token authorization. The token is expected to be passed using the HTTP Authorization request header, and is retrieved using the Login endpoint.

At any point in the interaction with OpsAPI, a HTTP status code of 401 in the response indicates that the bearer token may be invalid. Typically, a new Login attempt may resolve this issue.

  • At the begining of each "session", invoke the Login endpoint with:
    • A valid B2W Ops user-name and a password
      • The user-name corresponds to the Windows Account Name for a user in the B2W Operational suite.
      • The password corresponds to the the Windows Account password.
    • Or provide “clientid” and “clientSecret” values:
      • The clientId and clientSecret values corresponds to the values generated by the B2W Operational Suite in the Manage Api Key dialog.
      • This option appears when the B2W Ops system is setup in TID (Trimble Identity) mode, and executes API actions with System Administrator privileges.
  • Alternatively, when the B2W Ops system is setup in TID (Trimble Identity) mode, to executes API actions as a specific user:
    • Invoke the LoginWithTID endpoint with a valid Trimble ID Access Token
      • The Trimble ID Access Token must be retrieved from a user logging into the Trimble Identity system, and can then be passed into this API.
      • The identity stored in the Access Token corresponds to a user in the B2W Operational Suite who has logged into the B2W Operational Suite using that Trimble Identity previously.

On successful login, the response will contain a JWT, which contains the AccessToken. This AccessToken must be sent with all other endpoints.

API Documentation

The following sections provide detailed information regarding every functional area. Click on the section title to expand/collapse that section.

parameters.name: queryOptions
parameters.description: OData query options
parameters.example:
$orderby=Name asc
$select=Name
$skip=1&$top=1
$filter=startswith(BusinessUnitFullPathName, 'Organization\Temporary\Con')
$filter=contains(BusinessUnitFullPathName, 'Organization\Temporary\Con')

The query options may be combined to create compound queries.

post.summary: Create a new attachment based on the specified model.
post.description:
File Attachments MUST be uploaded with the content-type set to multipart/form-data, where the name in the content-disposition header is the description (not more than 100 characters) to be associated with the attachment.
Multiple files can be added in a single request; however, be aware of the maximum size limit imposed by IIS (typically 30 MB).
The application configuration file (web.config) may be used to increase/decreate the total size of the upload.
Here is the snippet of the configuration file relevant to the setting:
<requestLimits maxAllowedContentLength="10485760" />
The value is specified in Bytes; in this example the upload limit being set to 10 MB (10 * 1024 * 1024 = 10485760).
